hash_injection.description: DOM XSS via location.hash -- user-controlled hash value flows into dangerous sinks without sanitization

probes.description: Hash fragment injection into innerHTML sink -- hash value not sent to server but processed client-side

postmessage_sink.description: DOM XSS via postMessage -- missing origin validation allows cross-origin message injection into dangerous sinks

probes.description: Page registers message event listener without origin check -- attacker page can postMessage with malicious payload

url_source.description: DOM XSS via URL parameters -- location, URLSearchParams, or referrer flows into dangerous sinks

probes.description: JavaScript URI in URL parameter flowing to location.href or window.open sink

jquery_sink.description: DOM XSS via jQuery selector injection -- user input passed to $() creates HTML elements

probes.description: jQuery selector with user-controlled input -- $() with location data creates executable DOM nodes

open_redirect_dom.description: DOM-based open redirect -- user-controlled value flows to location assignment without validation

probes.description: Protocol-relative redirect via DOM sink -- parameter value assigned to window.location without origin check

document_domain.description: document.domain manipulation -- relaxing same-origin policy enables cross-subdomain attacks

probes.description: Page sets document.domain -- relaxes SOP allowing any subdomain to access this page DOM

trusted_types_default_policy.description: Trusted Types (TT) require sinks to receive TrustedHTML/TrustedScript objects. Apps create a 'default' policy that serves as a last-line sanitizer. If attacker can hijack default policy creation (e.g. early <script> injection in pre-TT region), they bypass TT enforcement.

probes.description: Detect Trusted Types in CSP / HTML. Look for `Content-Security-Policy: ... require-trusted-types-for 'script'` or `trustedTypes.createPolicy(`.
Find default policy. If `createPolicy('default', ...)` exists, inspect createHTML/createScript implementations. Pass-through (returns input unchanged) defeats TT entirely.

dompurify_mutation_xss.description: DOMPurify versions <3.0.5 had multiple mutation-XSS bypasses. Specific MathML / SVG namespaced elements re-parse after sanitization, mutating into XSS-executing markup. 2024-2025 research continues to find variants in Angular / React integrations.

probes.description: DOMPurify mutation XSS — namespace confusion across math/svg/html. After sanitization the browser re-parses and `onerror` becomes attribute on img.
noscript-context confusion — browser parses inside noscript when JS enabled differently than DOMPurify expects.
SVG self-closing handler — old DOMPurify variants miss the slash-attribute trick.

sanitizer_api_namespace_drift.description: The native Sanitizer API (Element.setHTML, Document.parseHTMLUnsafe) has different default allowlists than DOMPurify. Apps relying on Sanitizer API may allow MathML / SVG elements that re-parse to XSS in some browsers.

probes.description: MathML xlink:href javascript: — Sanitizer API may allow xlink in MathML namespace by default.
